title: myrcia tonii
intro_study: According to "International journal of molecular sciences", Myrcia tonii benefits may include hypoglycemic, anti-hemorrhagic and antioxidant activities.
parts: Myrcia tonii has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and stems, which are used to produce various compounds with potential health benefits. The leaves of Myrcia tonii contain flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots and stems of the plant are rich in diterpenes, such as myrciaphenone and myrciadienone, which have been found to exhibit antimicrobial and antitumor activities. Additionally, the plant's essential oils, obtained from its leaves and stems, contain compounds like limonene and beta-pinene, which have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
side_effects: Myrcia tonii can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing allergic reactions, including hives, itching, and difficulty breathing in some individuals. Prolonged use or excessive consumption of the plant may lead to gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and stomach pain. In severe cases, it can cause cardiovascular problems, including increased heart rate and blood pressure. Additionally, it may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, potentially exacerbating underlying health conditions.
